that would help to convict any

other shop is said to have offered

T. H. Steinbach, one of the three
union men under arrest, could not
be identified by A. II. Halvorsen,
when the latter was taken by a
policeman to the union machinists
hall to see if he could find the
man who struck him. Yet Stein
bach was soon afterward arrested
on warrant sworn out by Maan-
ger Fitzpatrick of the Union Iron
Works. The trial of Steinbach, E.
j. Graham and r. d. Wyant, union
men. charged with whipping strike
bre,txers will come up Thursday
morning bfeore Justice Hyde. The
men have secured bonds and re-
tanned Attorney Z. o. Conner to
look after their interests.
